summary.title: AEP Agent-as-Event-Operator Path Hardening
summary.description: Validation of AgentEventOperator hardening features including production truth binding via commit SHA, environment validation, agent context validation, and event structure validation. Ensures agents participating as event operators in AEP pipelines are governed and production-ready.

agentEventOperator.purpose: Adapts a TypedAgent for use as an event-oriented operator in AEP pipelines. Wraps agent processing with validation and production truth binding.

hardeningFeatures.description: Validates agent context completeness before processing events.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateAgentContext() checks for non-null context and non-empty tenantId.
validationRules: AgentContext tenantId must not be null
AgentContext tenantId must not be empty
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: AgentContextValidation nested test class with 4 tests

hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateEventStructure() checks for non-null and non-empty event map.
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: EventStructureValidation nested test class with 3 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Binds agent execution to commit SHA for production truth tracking.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: validateCommitShaForProduction() checks commitSha presence and format in production environment. commitSha and environment are set via setters.
validationRules: Production environment requires non-null, non-empty commit SHA
Non-production environments do not require commit SHA
No environment set defaults to non-production behavior
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: CommitShaBinding nested test class with 5 tests

hardeningFeatures.implementation: Environment is set via setEnvironment() and checked in validateCommitShaForProduction().
validationRules: Production environment enforces commit SHA requirement
Non-production environments allow execution without commit SHA
Environment value is case-sensitive for production check
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: Environment validation is tested through commit SHA binding tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Delegates event processing to the wrapped TypedAgent with proper error propagation.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: submit() calls agent.process(ctx, event) and maps AgentResult to output map. Failures propagate unchanged.
validationRules: Agent process method is called with context and event
Agent output is returned on success
Agent failures are propagated to caller
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: AgentExecution nested test class with 3 tests

testCategories.name: Agent Context Validation
testCategories.coverage: Rejects null context, rejects null tenant ID, rejects empty tenant ID, accepts valid context

testCategories.name: Event Structure Validation
testCategories.coverage: Rejects null event, rejects empty event, accepts non-empty event

testCategories.name: Commit SHA Binding
testCategories.coverage: Production requires commit SHA, production accepts valid commit SHA, rejects invalid commit SHA format, non-production does not require commit SHA, no environment does not require commit SHA

testCategories.name: Agent Execution
testCategories.coverage: Delegates to agent process, returns agent output on success, propagates agent failure

typedAgent.purpose: Typed agent interface for processing input to output

agentContext.purpose: Agent execution context with tenant scoping

deadLetterOperator.purpose: Dead letter operator for resilient event handling

productionTruthGuarantees: Agent execution is bound to specific commit SHA in production
Commit SHA format is validated (40-character hexadecimal)
Agent context is validated for tenant scoping
Event structure is validated before processing
securityConsiderations: Commit SHA binding prevents deployment of unverified agent code
Agent context validation ensures tenant scoping
Event structure validation prevents malformed input
notes: AgentEventOperator bridges agent-core processing model with AEP event pipeline
Commit SHA binding is enforced for production environment only
Agent context validation ensures tenant scoping
Event structure validation prevents malformed input
Test coverage includes all hardening features
Production truth is maintained through commit SHA and environment validation
